patch.value: GH-485: pm search rewrites quoted status:all hybrid queries into --status and drops keywords

Quoted hybrid queries beginning with an inline status: filter are rewritten into a --status flag whose value swallows the remaining keywords, leaving no positional keywords and failing the command. Reproduced on 2026.7.9.

pm search "status:all scene grounding" applies the status filter and treats remaining tokens as keywords; regression test covers inline-filter-first quoted queries; if a query is inline-filter-only, the error names the inline-filter syntax rather than a generic missing-keywords message

## Reproduction (verified on 2026.7.9, local build)

```bash
pm search "status:all scene grounding" --limit 5
# Error: Missing required argument keywords
# Recovery bundle shows: attempted_command: pm search --status "all scene grounding" --limit 5
```

The inline-filter rewriter consumes everything after `status:` into the flag value instead of stopping at the first whitespace token boundary.

## Impact
Breaks the documented duplicate-check workflow (`pm search "status:all <keywords>"` before create) that agents use to avoid re-filing items. Workaround: pass `--status all` explicitly and keep keywords positional.

## Upstream
https://github.com/unbraind/pm-cli/issues/485 (filed 2026-07-08 by unbraind while implementing an all-status duplicate-check workflow).

## Likely surface
Inline query-syntax parser from GH-157 remainder work (pm-ldr1) — the `status:`/`tag:` token rewrite path in search argument normalization.
